title: helianthus annuus subsp. jaegeri
intro: Helianthus annuus subsp. jaegeri, also known as the giant sunflower, is a subspecies of the common sunflower and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's flowers and seeds have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Helianthus annuus subsp. jaegeri include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, as well as its potential to act as a natural remedy for wounds and skin conditions. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be made by extracting the active compounds from the flowers and seeds through various methods like steam distillation or solvent extraction.
